title: Vocabulary Learning via Optimal Transport for Neural Machine Translation
abstract: The choice of token vocabulary affects the performance of machine translation. This paper aims to figure out what is a good vocabulary and whether we can find the optimal vocabulary without trial training by providing an alternative understanding of vocabulary from the perspective of information theory.

title: Grounded Language-Image Pre-training
abstract: This paper presents a grounded language-image pre-training (GLIP) model for learning object-level, language-aware, and semantic-rich visual representations. GLIP unifies object detection and phrase grounding for pre-training, achieving state-of-the-art results on various vision-language tasks.

title: Are Emergent Abilities of Large Language Models a Mirage?
abstract: Recent work claims that large language models display emergent abilities, transitioning seemingly instantaneously from not present to present at unforeseeable model scales. We present evidence suggesting that these emergent abilities are heavily dependent on the chosen metrics and may largely be a mirage.

title: A Multi-Stable Curved Line Shape Display
abstract: In this work, we propose a multi-stable curved line shape display capable of rendering various organic and non-uniform shapes. This expands the expressive capabilities of shape-changing interfaces beyond traditional grid-based pin arrays.

title: CycleResearcher: Improving Automated Research via Automated Review
abstract: This paper explores the feasibility of using open-source post-trained LLMs as autonomous agents capable of performing the full cycle of automated research and review, from literature review and manuscript preparation to peer review and paper refinement.

title: Momentum Contrast for Unsupervised Visual Representation Learning
abstract: We present Momentum Contrast (MoCo) for unsupervised visual representation learning. From a perspective on contrastive learning as dictionary look-up, we build a dynamic dictionary with a queue and a moving-averaged encoder. This enables building a large and consistent dictionary on-the-fly that facilitates contrastive unsupervised learning.

title: On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big?
abstract: We explore the risks of large language models, including environmental costs, financial costs, and the dangers of relying on static, massive datasets that encode biases. We argue for more careful consideration of the training data and the potential for these models to function as 'stochastic parrots'.

title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows
abstract: This paper presents a new vision Transformer, called Swin Transformer, that capably serves as a general-purpose backbone for computer vision. Challenges in adapting Transformer from language to vision arise from differences between the two domains, which we address with a hierarchical architecture whose representation is computed with Shifted windows.

title: Connecting the Dots: Multivariate Time Series Forecasting with Graph Neural Networks
abstract: Multivariate time series forecasting is critical in many domains. We propose a general graph neural network framework designed to uncover spatial-temporal relationships among multiple variables automatically without predefined graphs.

title: Masked Autoencoders Are Scalable Vision Learners
abstract: This paper shows that masked autoencoders (MAE) are scalable vision learners. Our asymmetric encoder-decoder architecture allows us to mask a high proportion of the input image, yielding a nontrivial and meaningful self-supervisory task.

title: Co-Writing with Opinionated Language Models Affects Users' Views
abstract: We investigate how writing with an opinionated language model affects users' own views. Through a controlled experiment, we found that participants shifted their written and underlying opinions to align with the language model's embedded biases.

title: Adding Conditional Control to Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
abstract: We present ControlNet, a neural network architecture to add spatial conditioning controls to large, pretrained text-to-image diffusion models. ControlNet locks the production-ready large diffusion models, and reuses their deep encoding layers pretrained with billions of images as a strong backbone.

title: Multi-grained Vision Language Pre-training: Aligning Texts with Visual Concepts
abstract: We propose a multi-grained vision language pre-training framework. Instead of simply relying on object detectors or global image-text alignment, our approach introduces fine-grained semantic alignment between text descriptions and localized visual concepts.

title: RT-1: Robotics Transformer for Real-World Control at Scale
abstract: We present RT-1, a robotics transformer model that takes images and natural language instructions and outputs tokenized camera and arm actions. It exhibits significant generalization across tasks, environments, and objects after being trained on a large-scale real-world robotics dataset.

title: Designing Interactive Systems for Prompt Engineering
abstract: As large language models become more capable, prompt engineering emerges as a critical user task. We present a novel interactive system that assists users in iteratively refining, managing, and evaluating prompts for complex generative tasks.

title: A Survey on Vision Transformer
abstract: Transformers have achieved great success in natural language processing. Recently, they have shown promising performance in computer vision. This paper comprehensively reviews the application of Transformers in computer vision tasks, detailing architectural variants and performance benchmarks.

title: Dense Text Retrieval based on Pre-trained Language Models
abstract: Dense retrieval based on pre-trained language models has become a new paradigm for text retrieval. We propose a novel training methodology that significantly improves the effectiveness of dense retrievers by mining hard negatives dynamically without increasing inference latency.

title: Poisoning Web-Scale Training Datasets is Practical
abstract: We demonstrate that poisoning large, web-scale training datasets is both practical and highly effective. We introduce a new class of poisoning attacks that can manipulate model outputs for specific triggers without degrading overall performance, bypassing standard anomaly detection.

title: LLMs as Research Tools: Applications and Evaluations in HCI Data Work
abstract: We evaluate how LLMs are being adopted to perform actual HCI research tasks like data collection, thematic analysis, and qualitative coding. The authors critically evaluate the methodological and ethical appropriateness of using LLMs in these research pipelines.

title: WavLM: Large-Scale Self-Supervised Pre-Training for Full Stack Speech Processing
abstract: We introduce WavLM, a large-scale self-supervised pre-training framework for speech processing. By jointly learning masked speech prediction and denoising, WavLM achieves state-of-the-art performance on a wide range of speech tasks including recognition, speaker verification, and separation.

title: Grounding and Evaluation for Large Language Models: Practical Challenges and Lessons Learned
abstract: This survey evaluates the practical challenges of LLMs, such as hallucination, ethical grounding, and evaluation constraints. We provide a comprehensive overview of techniques for grounding LLMs in verifiable external knowledge sources for enterprise AI systems.

title: Adaptive Multi-Modal Fusion for Robust Zero-Shot Learning
abstract: Zero-shot learning relies heavily on aligning visual and semantic spaces. We propose an adaptive multi-modal fusion network that dynamically weights textual, visual, and depth features to improve robustness against missing or noisy modalities in real-world deployments.

title: Towards Intersectional Fairness in AI
abstract: Current fairness metrics often fail to capture intersectional biases. We introduce a new framework for evaluating and mitigating bias across intersecting identity groups, demonstrating its effectiveness on several widely used benchmark datasets.

title: Visualizing the Inner Workings of Large Language Models
abstract: Understanding the decision-making process of large language models is challenging. We introduce an interactive visual analytics tool that allows researchers to explore attention heads, sequence mappings, and neuron activations across different transformer layers dynamically.

title: Evaluating the Cognitive Load of Real-Time AI Translation Tools
abstract: Real-time AI translation tools are becoming ubiquitous, yet their impact on user cognitive load during active conversation remains under-explored. We present a mixed-methods study evaluating mental demand when using concurrent AI translation interfaces.
